source_docs.title: HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads
HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads
HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads
HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads

oligos.name: Acrydite_primer (bead PCR handle)
oligos.role: Acrydite/disulfide-anchored capture primer polymerized into the unbarcoded hydrogel bead; carries the T7 promoter + SMART/ISPCR PCR handle onto which the split-pool sub-barcodes are annealed and extended.
oligos.notes: Synthesized as /5Acryd//iThioMC6-D/TTTTTTTT AATACGACTCACTATAGGG (T7 promoter) AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC (SMART handle). 5' Acrydite grafts to the polyacrylamide matrix; iThioMC6-D disulfide is DTT-cleavable to release the primer in-droplet.

oligos.name: BC1 sub-barcode primer (round 1, 96 variants)
oligos.role: First split-pool barcoding oligo; its 3' GTACTCTGCG anneals to the bead handle (…CGCAGAGTAC) and is extended by linear amplification to append BC1 + the BC2 landing linker.
oligos.notes: One of 96; e.g. GCAGTAGCTGTGTAGCAAGTGTACTCTGCG. Complement of GCAGTAGCTG (=CAGCTACTGC) becomes the inter-barcode linker on the bead.

oligos.name: BC2 sub-barcode primer (round 2, 96 variants)
oligos.role: Second split-pool barcoding oligo; anneals to the BC1-extended bead and appends BC2 + the BC3 landing linker.
oligos.notes: One of 96; e.g. AGGGTACTCGTTAGTTGGACGCAGTAGCTG. Complement of AGGGTACTCG (=CGAGTACCCT) becomes the next inter-barcode linker.

oligos.name: BC3 sub-barcode primer (round 3, RNA capture, 96 variants)
oligos.role: Third/terminal split-pool barcoding oligo for HyDrop-RNA; appends BC3, the 8 nt UMI and the poly(dT) mRNA capture sequence.
oligos.notes: One of 96; e.g. A30-NNNNNNNN-TGACCGTACT-AGGGTACTCG. The poly-A / UMI / BC3 are templated onto the bead so the final bead primer reads BC3-UMI(8)-poly(dT). (HyDrop-ATAC uses a distinct BC3 oligo, CCGAGCCCACGAGAC…AGGGTACTCG, appending a Tn5-adapter-complementary capture site instead.)

oligos.name: Final HyDrop-RNA bead primer (assembled, 136 bp)
oligos.role: Fully barcoded bead-bound RT primer that captures poly(A)+ mRNA and serves as the cell-indexing RT primer in the droplet.
oligos.notes: 30 bp total cell barcode (3x10). Whitelist = 884,736 barcodes.

oligos.name: TSO (template-switching oligo)
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo present in the in-droplet RT reaction; its 3' rGrGrG pairs with the untemplated C's added by Maxima RT to append the ISPCR handle to the cDNA.
oligos.notes: Ribo-G at 3' (rGrGrG folded to GGG). An LNA-modified variant was tested in one optimization trial.

oligos.name: TSO-P / SMART PCR primer
oligos.role: Single primer for post-RT IS-PCR (bulk cDNA amplification); anneals to the shared SMART handle at both ends of the template-switched cDNA.
oligos.notes: Not yet indexing; amplification drops the 5' T7 promoter, leaving product starting at AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC.

oligos.name: NEBNext Adapter for Illumina (TruSeq Y-adapter)
oligos.role: Fragmentation/ligation adapter added to the 3' end of fragmented cDNA, providing the TruSeq Read 1 priming site (i5/P5 arm added in the final PCR).
oligos.notes: Standard NEBNext/TruSeq hairpin (E7805) with an internal deoxyuracil cleaved by USER enzyme after ligation; ligated top-strand stub shown.

oligos.name: Hy-i7 indexed primer (HYi7_x, 16 variants)
oligos.role: i7 sample-index library PCR primer; anneals to the bead SMART handle and adds the P7/i7 arm. In HyDrop the i7 index read is sacrificed to read the cell barcode, so i7 is not used for sample demultiplexing.

oligos.name: Hyi5-TS (HYi5_TruSeq_x) indexed primer
oligos.role: i5 sample-index library PCR primer used for HyDrop-RNA after fragmentation; anneals to the ligated TruSeq adapter and adds the P5/i5 arm plus the TruSeq Read 1 site.
oligos.notes: Use a different i5 index per sample (i5 is the sample demultiplexing index for RNA libraries).

oligos.name: HyDrop_CustSeq_R2 (custom Read 2 primer)
oligos.role: Custom Read 2 sequencing primer that anneals just 5' of BC1 and reads the three cell barcodes + UMI.
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified custom read-2 primer for HyDrop-RNA.

oligos.name: HyDrop_CustSeq_Short (custom i7 read primer)
oligos.role: Custom index-1 (i7) sequencing primer for HyDrop-RNA.
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of the CTGTCCGCGG…AGAGTAC handle; reads the i7 index.

oligos.name: dN-SMRT primer
oligos.role: Random-primed second-strand synthesis primer used only in the Bst 2.0 / Klenow optimization trials (alternative to TSO/IS-PCR).
oligos.notes: Sequence not disclosed in the provided documents; carries the SMART handle with a 3' random-N tail (added to 2.5 uM). Not used in the final GTP/PEG protocol.

oligos.name: Anti-Acrydite_FAM (QC probe)
oligos.role: FAM fluorescent QC probe against the acrydite handle stub on unbarcoded beads (baseline bead fluorescence).

oligos.name: Anti-RNA_FAM (QC probe)
oligos.role: FAM fluorescent QC probe against the poly(dT) capture site on HyDrop-RNA beads.

oligos.name: Anti-BC1_FAM (QC probe)
oligos.role: FAM probe complementary to one of the 96 BC1 sub-barcodes (sub-barcode purity QC).

oligos.name: Anti-BC2_FAM (QC probe)
oligos.role: FAM probe complementary to one of the 96 BC2 sub-barcodes.

oligos.name: Anti-BC3_FAM (QC probe)
oligos.role: FAM probe complementary to one of the 96 BC3 sub-barcodes.

final_library.source_label: HyDrop-RNA final dual-indexed library (Supplementary file 3 / eLife-73971-supp3)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT -[i7 SAMPLE_INDEX:10]- CTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC -[BC1 CELL_BARCODE:10]- CAGCTACTGC -[BC2 CELL_BARCODE:10]- CGAGTACCCT -[BC3 CELL_BARCODE:10]- [UMI:8] -TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT- [CDNA] -AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT- [i5 SAMPLE_INDEX:10] -GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT -3'
annotation_lines: CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT = P7 flow-cell / i7 primer landing (Hy-i7)
[SAMPLE_INDEX:10] = i7 sample index (read by custom i7 primer; not used for demux)
CTGTCCGCGGAAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC = SMART/ISPCR bead handle + Read 2 primer site
[CELL_BARCODE:10] (BC1) - CAGCTACTGC (linker) - [CELL_BARCODE:10] (BC2) - CGAGTACCCT (linker) - [CELL_BARCODE:10] (BC3) = 30 bp split-pool cell barcode with inter-barcode linkers
AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT = ligated NEBNext/TruSeq adapter + Read 1 primer site
GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT = P5 flow-cell arm (rev-comp of AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC)

evidence.locator: HyDrop-RNA final dual-indexed library (Supplementary file 3 / eLife-73971-supp3)

library_generation.title: mRNA capture in droplet
library_generation.summary: Cells are lysed inside droplets and poly(A)+ mRNA anneals to the poly(dT) tail of the released, uniquely barcoded bead primer.
library_generation.note: DTT in the RT mix cleaves the disulfide linker, releasing the barcoded primer from the dissolving hydrogel; bead lysis buffer lyses the cell simultaneously.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (first strand)
library_generation.summary: Maxima H- RT extends the bead primer along the mRNA, producing barcoded first-strand cDNA and adding untemplated C's at the 3' end.
library_generation.note: In-droplet RT: 42C 90 min with 11 cycles of 50C/42C, GTP + PEG-8000 supplemented to stabilize the template-switch complex and act as a crowding agent.

library_generation.title: Template switching
library_generation.summary: The TSO anneals via its 3' rGGG to the untemplated CCC and RT copies it, appending the ISPCR/SMART handle to the cDNA 3' end.

library_generation.title: Emulsion break + IS-PCR amplification
library_generation.summary: Droplets are broken and pooled, and cDNA is bulk-amplified with the single TSO-P primer against the shared SMART handles at both ends.
library_generation.note: ExoI treatment removes unused barcode primers before ISPCR (13 cycles, KAPA HiFi). Amplification drops the 5' T7 promoter, so product starts at AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTAC.

library_generation.title: NEBNext fragmentation + dA-tailing
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is enzymatically fragmented and dA-tailed (NEBNext Ultra II FS).

library_generation.title: Adapter ligation (NEBNext/TruSeq) + USER
library_generation.summary: The NEBNext Illumina Y-adapter is ligated to the fragmented cDNA and the adapter uracil is cleaved by USER enzyme.
library_generation.note: Provides the TruSeq Read 1 priming site on the cDNA (i5/P5) end.

library_generation.title: Dual-index PCR (final library)
library_generation.summary: Hy-i7 (binds bead SMART handle) and Hyi5-TruSeq (binds ligated adapter) primers add the P7/i7 and P5/i5 arms to make the sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: 13 cycles KAPA HiFi; a distinct i5 index per sample (i5 = sample demux; i7 read is spent reading the cell barcode side).

library_sequencing.note: Reads 50 nt of the 3' cDNA insert. The Read 1 primer is the reverse complement of the ligated NEBNext/TruSeq stub (AGATCGGAAGAGC...AGAGTGT) on the top strand; its 3' end sits at the cDNA-proximal edge and extends into the insert, so the top strand is the template.
library_sequencing.diagram:
<------- Read 1 : cDNA insert (50 nt)
         3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGCAGCACATCCCTTTCTCACA-5'   TruSeq Read 1 primer
5'-...(cDNA)AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT[i5:10]GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT-3'   (top)
3'-...(cDNA)TCTAGCCTTCTCGCAGCACATCCCTTTCTCACA[i5:10]CACATCTAGAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA-5'   (bottom)

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 10 nt i7 index. The custom primer (5'-GTACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTTCCGCGGACAG-3') is the reverse complement of the CTGTCCGCGG...AGAGTAC bead handle; it anneals to the top strand just 3' of the i7 and reads leftward into it. In HyDrop the i7 read is NOT used for sample demultiplexing.

Reads the 10 nt i5 index from the P5/TruSeq side. i5 is the true sample-demultiplexing index for HyDrop-RNA (a different i5 per sample). On the NextSeq2000 the i5 is read from the resynthesized bottom strand.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i5 index primer
                                              <------- read i5 (10 nt)
5'-...AGATCGGAAGAGCGTCGTGTAGGGAAAGAGTGT[i5:10]GTGTAGATCTCGGTGGTCGCCGTATCATT-3'   (top)
3'-...TCTAGCCTTCTCGCAGCACATCCCTTTCTCACA[i5:10]CACATCTAGAGCCACCAGCGGCATAGTAA-5'   (bottom)

library_sequencing.note: Reads BC1(10) + CAGCTACTGC(10) + BC2(10) + CGAGTACCCT(10) + BC3(10) + UMI(8) = 58 nt. The custom primer is identical to the CTGTCCGCGG...AGAGTAC bead handle on the top strand, so it anneals to the bottom strand and copies it, emitting the barcodes in top-strand sense. Parsed by STARsolo CB_UMI_Complex.

title: HyDrop-RNA
description: HyDrop-RNA is an open-source droplet-microfluidic single-cell RNA-seq assay built on dissolvable, barcoded polyacrylamide hydrogel beads. Each bead carries a three-round split-pool cell barcode (96x96x96 = 884,736 combinations), an 8 nt UMI, and a poly(dT) capture sequence. Inside droplets, cells are lysed and poly(A)+ mRNA is reverse transcribed on the bead primer with template switching (Drop-seq/SMART-style chemistry, GTP/PEG-supplemented); barcoded cDNA is pooled, amplified by IS-PCR, then fragmented and ligated to a NEBNext/TruSeq adapter and dual-indexed. Read 1 reads the 3' cDNA insert, Read 2 reads the three linker-separated cell barcodes plus UMI, and the i7/i5 index reads carry the sample index.

reference.label: HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads

original_publication.title: HyDrop enables droplet-based single-cell ATAC-seq and single-cell RNA-seq using dissolvable hydrogel beads

throughput.summary: 9508 single-nuclei transcriptomes from flash-frozen mouse cortex in a single run at ~65% cell recovery; median ~3389 UMIs / 1658 genes per cell (~60% sequencing saturation). Species-mixing: 1235 human + 846 mouse cells, median 1439 UMIs / 904 genes. 973 FAC-sorted Drosophila neurons recovered from ~1500 input.
throughput.cells: 9508 mouse cortex nuclei per run; >90% bead-in-droplet occupancy, ~65% final cell recovery
throughput.rna: median ~3389–3404 UMIs and ~1658–1662 genes per cell (mouse cortex); sequencing efficiency 7.44% of reads become mapped UMIs
throughput.dna: 884,736 (96x96x96) cell barcodes; 30 bp barcode + 8 nt UMI

publication.statistical_model: Not explicitly stated; droplet UMI counts (UMIs deduplicate PCR duplicates). Bead-in-droplet loading exceeds Poisson encapsulation statistics via close-packed ordering of deformable beads (Abate et al., 2009).

other.label: Bead
other.value: ~50 um dissolvable polyacrylamide with disulfide crosslinkers; 12 uM acrydite primer optimal

other.label: Microfluidics
other.value: Droplet Genomics Onyx platform, HyDrop High Aspect 65/80 chip, RAN Biotech EA-008 / HFE-7500 oil

data_processing.summary: Barcode reads (Read 2) are first trimmed of the inter-sub-barcode linear-amplification linkers, then reads are mapped and cell-demultiplexed with STARsolo in CB_UMI_Complex mode against the 884,736-barcode whitelist (1 bp mismatch); the resulting UMI count matrix is analyzed in Scanpy with Scrublet doublet removal, normalization/PCA/UMAP, and marker-gene cluster annotation.
